frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing FLS Expanded Video Tutorial for Task 5: Intracorporeal Suture
Hands visible loading needle with suture outside the trainer box
First instrument entering trainer field - timer starts, can see Penrose drain with marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean entry into trainer, drain visible with marking system

frame_analyses.description: Needle being passed through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise needle placement through first mark

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through first puncture site
Beginning surgeon's knot formation - creating double wrap for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot technique with double throw

frame_analyses.description: Completing second throw with hand switching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand switching technique between throws

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being completed with proper hand switching
Using scissors to cut suture ends close to knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate suture tail length being maintained

frame_analyses.description: Task completed with knot secured and drain slit closed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows secure knot with good drain closure

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Good single throw with proper hand switching
Final throw completed with appropriate hand switching

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent demonstration of proper FLS Task 5 technique with accurate suture placement, correct knot sequence, and appropriate hand switching between throws.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly faster transitions between knot throws to improve efficiency
